content.practice: train on the same serialization used at runtime
test the same path that ships
restore as part of the schedule
safe to repeat without side effects

content.text: an LLM agent maintaining a repository
an engineer validating a compose stack
a reviewer checking a risky refactor
a dataset curator preparing a holdout split
a runtime owner debugging slow inference
a product engineer integrating resource APIs
a release reviewer checking acceptance gates
a security auditor reviewing access patterns
a new contributor reading the codebase
a DevOps engineer tuning deployment knobs
a data scientist evaluating model drift
a technical writer updating API docs
a site reliability engineer on call
a compliance officer checking audit trails
a frontend developer consuming agent output
tie the answer to one measurable outcome
state the default before the exception
avoid claiming success without a check
prefer typed interfaces over ad hoc shell steps
cite the specific file or line when possible
limit the response to three concrete steps
ask for clarification if the request is ambiguous
return a JSON action, never prose outside it
confirm before any write or mutation
preserve user privacy in every output
ground claims in the provided context only
flag any assumption that could be wrong
connect the design to serving quality
state what should not be optimized first
compare the current approach to the alternative
identify the smallest safe first step
trace the failure from symptom to root cause
list prerequisites before the main task

content.template: Explain how the assistant should handle {boundary}.
State the safe default when a user asks for {boundary}.
Describe the refusal and redirect policy for {boundary}.
Explain why unrestricted access to {boundary} is unsafe.
Describe the audit trail expectation for {boundary}.
Explain the data residency rule for {boundary}.

content.text: ask for explicit confirmation and a typed mutation payload
limit the workflow to authorized resource APIs
return a boundary explanation and stop before the write
suggest a read-only or preview-only alternative
escalate to a human reviewer with audit logging
return a synthetic or anonymized substitute
offer a time-limited temporary access grant
redirect to a self-service privacy portal
document the request and defer to business hours

content.subject: the difference between notes and media in kjxlkj
content.answer: Notes store editable markdown content. Media store uploaded files plus metadata and can be attached to notes.

content.subject: what `/api/resources/search` returns
content.answer: It returns JSON search results with resource payloads plus paging cursors.

content.subject: when kjxlkj writes require confirmation
content.answer: Create and update operations should be confirmed before the assistant executes them.

content.subject: what history means in kjxlkj
content.answer: History is the immutable sequence of saved snapshots for one resource.

content.subject: why prompt and training format must match
content.answer: If training and inference serialization differ, the model learns the wrong continuation boundary.

content.subject: why raw generation must be evaluated directly
content.answer: Proxy fallbacks can hide model failures and produce inflated quality claims.

content.subject: what retrieval should do for a 1024-token model
content.answer: Retrieval should select short relevant context instead of pushing the full history into one prompt.

content.subject: why typed tools are preferred
content.answer: Typed tools expose clear arguments, validation, and side-effect boundaries.

content.subject: what a plan action represents
content.answer: A visible outline of steps the assistant intends to take before making tool calls.

content.subject: why multi-turn trajectories help small models
content.answer: They teach the model to observe results and revise instead of guessing once.

content.subject: what the Chinchilla gap means for scratch-60m
content.answer: The model sees far fewer tokens than compute-optimal, so data quality matters more.

content.subject: why agentic rows use explicit tool names in plans
content.answer: It grounds the plan in executable actions and makes evaluation objective.

content.subject: what workspace-safe means for safety_scope
content.answer: The content is safe to use in local development without leaking secrets.

content.subject: why deduplication happens before splitting
content.answer: Splitting first would place near-duplicates in train and holdout, inflating scores.

content.subject: what behavioral_threshold tracks
content.answer: The minimum raw holdout pass rate required to accept a training run.

content.subject: why fixed eval checks artifact existence
content.answer: It prevents accepting a model that was never exported or has missing metadata.

content.subject: what gradient_accumulation simulates
content.answer: It increases effective batch size without raising peak memory.

content.subject: why cosine LR decay is used
content.answer: It smoothly reduces the learning rate so late steps still refine weights.

content.subject: what RMSNorm replaces
content.answer: It replaces LayerNorm with a root-mean-square normalization that avoids learnable bias.

content.subject: why grouped-query attention helps inference
content.answer: It reduces KV cache memory by sharing key and value heads across query heads.

content.subject: what SwiGLU does in the feed-forward layer
content.answer: It uses a gated activation for better expressiveness than ReLU or GELU.

content.subject: why the tokenizer trains on the train split only
content.answer: Training on validation or holdout would leak test information into the vocabulary.

content.subject: what a preference pair teaches via DPO
content.answer: It teaches the model to prefer chosen responses over rejected ones without a separate reward model.

content.subject: why competency gates use raw generation
content.answer: Supervised or fallback answers would hide true model capability.

content.subject: what the agent loop does after a tool_call
content.answer: It executes the tool, appends the observation, and prompts the model again.

content.subject: why confirmation is required for kjxlkj mutations
content.answer: It prevents accidental data loss or unauthorized changes.

content.subject: what memory.search returns
content.answer: It returns previously stored user preferences or facts relevant to the query.

content.subject: why shell.exec requires workspace-relative paths
content.answer: It prevents the agent from accessing system files outside the sandbox.

content.subject: what web.fetch is used for
content.answer: It retrieves public web pages for grounding or reference.

content.subject: why fs.list is safer than globbing
content.answer: It returns explicit directory contents without shell expansion risks.

content.subject: what resource.preview_markdown does
content.answer: It renders markdown safely for review before publishing.

content.subject: why history windows are bounded
content.answer: Unbounded history would exceed the model context limit.

content.subject: what visibility rules protect
content.answer: They prevent private notes from being exposed through search or summaries.

content.subject: why search kinds exist
content.answer: They let the user filter results by note, media, or all resources.

content.subject: what alias means for a kjxlkj resource
content.answer: A human-readable identifier that can be used instead of the internal ref.

content.subject: why is_private defaults matter
content.answer: They set the safest visibility state when the user does not specify one.

content.subject: what a holdout split evaluates
content.answer: It evaluates generalization on unseen tasks, not memorization.

content.subject: why JSON validity is tracked separately from pass rate
content.answer: A model can produce valid JSON that is completely wrong for the task.

content.subject: what bucket rates reveal
content.answer: They show per-task performance so aggregate scores do not hide regressions.

content.subject: why the plan action has no hidden thought field
content.answer: All reasoning must be observable and evaluable.

content.constraint: Return the correct tool, keep paths workspace-relative.
Prefer the typed tool call instead of prose.
Keep the action JSON minimal and valid.
Plan first, then call the tool.
Observe the result before the final answer.
Revise the plan if the first tool fails.
Chain two tools when the task requires it.
